ORA-00001.cause: An UPDATE or INSERT statement attempted to insert a duplicate key. For Trusted Oracle configured in DBMS MAC mode, you may see this message if a duplicate entry exists at a different level.
ORA-00001.action: Either remove the unique restriction or do not insert the key.

ORA-00017.message: session requested to set trace event
ORA-00017.cause: Another session requested that this session set a trace event.
ORA-00017.action: This is an internal message. Contact Oracle Support if it occurs frequently.

ORA-00018.cause: All session state objects are in use.
ORA-00018.action: Increase the value of the SESSIONS initialization parameter.

ORA-00019.message: maximum number of session licenses exceeded
ORA-00019.action: Increase the value of the LICENSE_MAX_SESSIONS initialization parameter.

ORA-00020.cause: All process state objects are in use.
ORA-00020.action: Increase the value of the PROCESSES initialization parameter.

ORA-00028.cause: A privileged user has killed your session.

ORA-00031.cause: The session specified in an ALTER SYSTEM KILL SESSION command cannot be killed immediately (it is rolling back or blocked on a network operation), and has been marked to be killed when the rollback is complete.
ORA-00031.action: Wait for the session to complete its rollback.

ORA-00054.message: resource busy and acquire with NOWAIT specified or timeout expired
ORA-00054.cause: The resource is being held by another user, and NOWAIT was specified or the timeout expired.
ORA-00054.action: Retry the command. If the error persists, investigate blocking sessions using V$LOCK and V$SESSION.

ORA-00055.message: maximum number of DML locks exceeded
ORA-00055.cause: The number of DML locks required exceeds the number allowed by the DML_LOCKS parameter.
ORA-00055.action: Increase the value of DML_LOCKS or reduce the number of tables locked per transaction.

ORA-00056.message: DDL lock on object '%s.%s' is already held
ORA-00056.cause: A DDL lock on the object is already held by another session.
ORA-00056.action: Wait for the DDL lock to be released or identify and kill the blocking session.

ORA-00060.message: deadlock detected while waiting for resource
ORA-00060.cause: Two or more processes are waiting for resources locked by each other, resulting in a deadlock.
ORA-00060.action: Retry the transaction. Review transaction logic to acquire locks in consistent order. Check the trace file written to the user dump destination.

ORA-00100.cause: A SELECT INTO statement returned no rows, or your program referenced an uninitialized row in a table.
ORA-00100.action: Check that the correct WHERE clause is specified. Use explicit cursor loops rather than SELECT INTO when zero rows are possible.

ORA-00200.message: control file could not be created
ORA-00200.cause: It was not possible to create the control file.
ORA-00200.action: Check that the device is online. Check the alert log for additional error information.

ORA-00201.message: control file version %s incompatible with ORACLE version %s
ORA-00201.cause: An attempt was made to access a control file that was created by a different (and incompatible) version of Oracle.
ORA-00201.action: Either use a control file compatible with this version of Oracle, or use the version of Oracle that is compatible with this control file.

ORA-00205.message: error in identifying control file, check alert log for more info
ORA-00205.cause: The system could not find a control file of the specified name and size.
ORA-00205.action: Check that all control files are online and that they are the same files that the system was last started with. Check the alert log for more information.

ORA-00210.message: cannot open the specified control file
ORA-00210.cause: It was not possible to open the control file.
ORA-00210.action: Check that the device is online, and that the control file specified exists.

ORA-00257.message: archiver error. Connect internal only, until freed
ORA-00257.cause: The archiver process received an error while trying to archive a redo log. The archive destination device may be out of space.
ORA-00257.action: Check the alert log for details. Free up space on the archive log destination or add more destination space. Connect as SYSDBA to investigate.

ORA-00312.cause: This message reports the filename for details of another error.
ORA-00312.action: See the associated errors for a description of the problem.

ORA-00313.message: open failed for members of log group %s of thread %s
ORA-00313.cause: The online log cannot be opened.
ORA-00313.action: See the accompanying errors. The log may need to be archived or cleared.

ORA-00333.message: redo log read error block %s count %s
ORA-00333.cause: An I/O error was encountered on a redo log.
ORA-00333.action: Make the redo log available. Check for OS-level I/O errors.

ORA-00338.message: log %s of thread %s is more recent than control file
ORA-00338.cause: The log sequence number in the control file is lower than the one in the log file.
ORA-00338.action: Restore a current copy of the control file or perform database recovery.

ORA-00341.message: log %s of thread %s, wrong log # %s in header
ORA-00341.cause: The header of the redo log file has a different log sequence number than what is stored in the control file.
ORA-00341.action: Restore from backup. Contact Oracle Support.

ORA-00361.message: cannot remove last log member %s for group %s
ORA-00361.cause: An attempt was made to drop the last member of an online redo log group.
ORA-00361.action: Each log group must have at least one member. Add a new member before removing this one.

ORA-00376.message: file %s cannot be read at this time
ORA-00376.cause: An attempt was made to read from a file that is not accessible.
ORA-00376.action: Bring the file online before reading it.

ORA-00439.cause: The feature is not enabled for this Oracle installation.
ORA-00439.action: Do not use this feature or obtain the appropriate Oracle license that includes it.

ORA-00494.message: enqueue [%s] held for too long (over %s seconds) by inst %s, osid %s
ORA-00494.cause: A process has held an enqueue for an unusual amount of time, probably due to a hung process.
ORA-00494.action: Check the process identified. Kill it if it is hung.

ORA-00600.cause: This is a generic internal error number that means a program error was encountered. This indicates a bug in Oracle.
ORA-00600.action: Report the error to Oracle Support with the full ORA-00600 arguments and the trace file. Do not attempt to diagnose internally.

ORA-00604.message: error occurred at recursive SQL level %s
ORA-00604.cause: An error occurred while processing a recursive SQL statement (a statement applying to internal dictionary tables).
ORA-00604.action: If the situation described in the next error on the stack can be corrected, do so. Otherwise contact Oracle Support.

ORA-00607.message: Internal error occurred while making a change to a data block
ORA-00607.cause: An internal error occurred while changing a data block.
ORA-00607.action: Report this error to Oracle Support along with the trace file.

ORA-00700.cause: An internal error for Oracle Support only.
ORA-00700.action: Contact Oracle Support with the trace file.

ORA-00704.cause: An error occurred during bootstrap of the database.
ORA-00704.action: Examine the other messages on the stack to diagnose the problem.

ORA-00900.cause: The SQL statement entered is not a valid SQL statement.

ORA-00901.cause: The CREATE command was not followed by a recognized keyword.
ORA-00901.action: Check the Oracle documentation for the correct CREATE statement syntax.

ORA-00903.cause: An invalid table name was given in the SQL statement.
ORA-00903.action: Check the table name for spelling errors and ensure it follows Oracle naming conventions.

ORA-00904.cause: An invalid column name was used. The column does not exist or the name is misspelled.
ORA-00904.action: Check the column name spelling. Use DESC table_name or SELECT * FROM ALL_TAB_COLUMNS to confirm the column name.

ORA-00905.cause: A required keyword is missing from a SQL statement.
ORA-00905.action: Check the SQL syntax. A keyword like SELECT, FROM, WHERE, or INTO may be missing.

ORA-00907.cause: A left parenthesis was not matched by a right parenthesis in a SQL statement.
ORA-00907.action: Check the SQL statement for unmatched parentheses.

ORA-00911.cause: A special character was found that is not permitted in identifiers.
ORA-00911.action: Remove the special character or quote the identifier.

ORA-00917.cause: A comma was expected in a SQL statement but was not found.
ORA-00917.action: Check the SQL statement for a missing comma in a column list or VALUES clause.

ORA-00918.cause: A column name used in a join exists in more than one table.
ORA-00918.action: Prefix the column name with the table alias: table_alias.column_name.

ORA-00920.cause: An invalid relational operator was used in a WHERE clause.
ORA-00920.action: Use valid relational operators: =, !=, <>, >, <, >=, <=, LIKE, IN, BETWEEN, IS NULL.

ORA-00921.action: Check the SQL statement for completeness. A clause or expression may be missing at the end.

ORA-00923.message: FROM keyword not found where expected
ORA-00923.cause: The FROM keyword was missing or misspelled in a SELECT statement.
ORA-00923.action: Correct the SELECT statement to include the FROM keyword before the table name.

ORA-00924.cause: The keyword BY is missing in an ORDER BY or GROUP BY clause.
ORA-00924.action: Check the ORDER BY or GROUP BY clause for the missing BY keyword.

ORA-00926.cause: An INSERT statement is missing the VALUES keyword.
ORA-00926.action: Add VALUES to the INSERT statement: INSERT INTO table_name (columns) VALUES (values);

ORA-00928.cause: The SELECT keyword is missing where it is expected, often in a subquery or CREATE AS SELECT.
ORA-00928.action: Add the SELECT keyword in the appropriate location.

ORA-00933.cause: The SQL statement is not properly ended or has an extra clause appended.
ORA-00933.action: Remove the trailing clause (e.g. ORDER BY is not allowed with DELETE or UPDATE in SQL).

ORA-00934.message: group function is not allowed here
ORA-00934.cause: A group function (SUM, AVG, COUNT, etc.) was used in a WHERE clause or other context where it is not allowed.
ORA-00934.action: Move the group function to a HAVING clause or use it in the SELECT list instead.

ORA-00936.cause: A required expression is missing from the SQL statement.
ORA-00936.action: Check the SQL for incomplete SELECT, WHERE, or ORDER BY clauses.

ORA-00937.cause: A SELECT statement contains both non-aggregate columns and aggregate functions without a GROUP BY clause.
ORA-00937.action: Add a GROUP BY clause listing all non-aggregate columns, or remove non-aggregate columns from the SELECT.

ORA-00938.cause: A SQL function was called with too few arguments.
ORA-00938.action: Check the function documentation and provide all required arguments.

ORA-00939.cause: A SQL function was called with too many arguments.
ORA-00939.action: Check the function documentation and remove the extra arguments.

ORA-00942.message: table or view does not exist
ORA-00942.cause: The table or view referenced in the SQL statement does not exist, or you do not have permission to access it.
ORA-00942.action: Verify the object name and schema. Grant SELECT privilege if the object exists but is not accessible. Use ALL_TABLES to check object existence.

ORA-00947.cause: An INSERT statement has fewer values than the column list specifies.
ORA-00947.action: Ensure the VALUES list has the same number of entries as the column list.

ORA-00948.cause: The FORCE option of a CREATE VIEW statement is no longer supported for this type of view.
ORA-00948.action: Remove the FORCE keyword or create the underlying objects first.

ORA-00955.message: name is already used by an existing object
ORA-00955.cause: An attempt was made to create a database object with a name that is already used by another object.
ORA-00955.action: Use a different name for the new object, or drop the existing object first.

ORA-00959.cause: A statement referenced a tablespace that does not exist in the database.
ORA-00959.action: Check the tablespace name. Use SELECT TABLESPACE_NAME FROM DBA_TABLESPACES to list available tablespaces.

ORA-00980.message: synonym translation is no longer valid
ORA-00980.cause: The synonym used refers to a database object that no longer exists.
ORA-00980.action: Drop and recreate the synonym pointing to the correct object, or recreate the referenced object.

ORA-01000.cause: A host language program attempted to open too many cursors. The maximum number is specified by the OPEN_CURSORS initialization parameter.
ORA-01000.action: Increase the OPEN_CURSORS initialization parameter or close cursors that are no longer needed in the application. Investigate cursor leaks.

ORA-01002.cause: A fetch was performed on a cursor after the last row was returned or after the cursor was closed.
ORA-01002.action: Ensure FETCH operations occur before cursor exhaustion. Reopen cursor if needed.

ORA-01012.cause: A call to Oracle was made without first connecting to the database.
ORA-01012.action: Establish a database connection before executing SQL.

ORA-01017.cause: An invalid username or password was entered in an attempt to log on to Oracle.
ORA-01017.action: Enter a valid username and password combination. Verify the correct database is being targeted.

ORA-01031.cause: An attempt was made to perform a database operation without the necessary privileges.
ORA-01031.action: Ask your DBA to grant you the required privilege, such as CREATE SESSION, SELECT ANY TABLE, or the specific object privilege needed.

ORA-01033.message: ORACLE initialization or shutdown in progress
ORA-01033.cause: An attempt was made to log on while Oracle is being started up or shut down.
ORA-01033.action: Wait for startup or shutdown to complete, then retry.

ORA-01034.cause: Oracle was not started up. Most common error when the Oracle background processes are not running.
ORA-01034.action: Start the Oracle instance using STARTUP from SQL*Plus connected as SYSDBA.

ORA-01036.cause: An attempt was made to bind a variable that has an invalid name or number.
ORA-01036.action: Check the SQL statement and bind variable names. Ensure host variable names match the placeholders in the SQL.

ORA-01045.message: user %s lacks CREATE SESSION privilege; logon denied
ORA-01045.cause: The user does not have CREATE SESSION privilege.
ORA-01045.action: Grant CREATE SESSION to the user: GRANT CREATE SESSION TO username;

ORA-01078.cause: An error occurred while reading or processing the initialization parameter file.
ORA-01078.action: Check the PFILE or SPFILE for syntax errors. Check the alert log for the specific parameter causing the failure.

ORA-01089.message: immediate shutdown in progress - no operations are permitted
ORA-01089.cause: An immediate shutdown is in progress.
ORA-01089.action: Wait until shutdown is complete, then restart the database.

ORA-01113.cause: An attempt was made to open a database file that needs media recovery.
ORA-01113.action: Perform media recovery on the file using RECOVER DATAFILE before opening.

ORA-01114.message: IO error writing block to file %s (block # %s)
ORA-01114.cause: The device on which the file resides is probably offline.
ORA-01114.action: Restore access to the device. Check OS-level disk errors in the alert log and OS logs.

ORA-01157.message: cannot identify/lock data file %s - see DBWR trace file
ORA-01157.cause: The background process DBWR was unable to find or lock a data file.
ORA-01157.action: Check the DBWR trace file for details. Ensure all data files are available and that the OS file permissions are correct.

ORA-01186.cause: A data file failed the verification test during startup.
ORA-01186.action: Restore the file from backup and recover.

ORA-01187.message: cannot read from file %s because it failed verification tests
ORA-01187.cause: The file failed verification tests and cannot be read.
ORA-01187.action: Restore the data file from backup and perform recovery.

ORA-01219.message: database not open: queries allowed on fixed tables/views only
ORA-01219.cause: The database is not open for read/write. It may be in MOUNT state.
ORA-01219.action: Open the database: ALTER DATABASE OPEN;

ORA-01400.cause: An INSERT or UPDATE statement attempted to insert a NULL value into a column with a NOT NULL constraint.
ORA-01400.action: Provide a non-NULL value for the column, or modify the table definition to allow NULLs if appropriate.

ORA-01401.message: inserted value too large for column
ORA-01401.cause: The value being inserted is too large for the column.
ORA-01401.action: Either shorten the value or increase the column size using ALTER TABLE.

ORA-01403.cause: In a SELECT INTO statement, no rows were returned. In a FETCH statement, all rows have been fetched.
ORA-01403.action: Ensure the query returns at least one row, or add exception handling for NO_DATA_FOUND in PL/SQL.

ORA-01406.cause: The value fetched from a column was too large to fit into the host variable.
ORA-01406.action: Increase the size of the host variable to hold the maximum column value, or use SUBSTR to limit the fetched length.

ORA-01408.cause: An attempt was made to create an index on a set of columns that is already indexed.
ORA-01408.action: Drop the duplicate index or use the existing index.

ORA-01422.message: exact fetch returns more than requested number of rows
ORA-01422.cause: A SELECT INTO statement returned more than one row.
ORA-01422.action: Use an explicit cursor or add WHERE clause conditions to restrict the result to one row.

ORA-01426.cause: Evaluation of an expression gives a result that is out of the valid number range.
ORA-01426.action: Review the expression for values that may exceed the column or variable definition.

ORA-01427.message: single-row subquery returns more than one row
ORA-01427.cause: A subquery used in a single-value context (=, !=, >, etc.) returned multiple rows.
ORA-01427.action: Use IN or EXISTS for multi-row comparisons, or add a WHERE clause to the subquery to return at most one row.

ORA-01450.cause: The combined length of the columns specified in the CREATE INDEX statement exceeds the maximum.
ORA-01450.action: Reduce the number or size of the indexed columns, or use a function-based index on shorter representations.

ORA-01461.message: can bind a LONG value only for insert into a LONG column
ORA-01461.cause: An attempt was made to bind a LONG value but the target column is not LONG.
ORA-01461.action: Use a CLOB column instead of LONG, or bind a shorter value. Migrate LONG columns to CLOB where possible.

ORA-01476.cause: An expression attempted to divide a number by zero.
ORA-01476.action: Add a CASE or NULLIF check: NULLIF(divisor, 0) before dividing, or add a WHERE clause to exclude zero values.

ORA-01480.message: trailing null missing from STR bind value
ORA-01480.cause: An OCI string bind value was not null-terminated.
ORA-01480.action: Ensure all string bind variables in OCI or Pro*C code are properly null-terminated.

ORA-01481.cause: An invalid format model was used in a TO_NUMBER or TO_CHAR function.
ORA-01481.action: Check the Oracle number format model documentation and correct the format string.

ORA-01502.message: index '%s.%s' or partition of such index is in unusable state
ORA-01502.cause: An attempt was made to access an index that has been marked unusable.
ORA-01502.action: Rebuild the index: ALTER INDEX owner.index_name REBUILD; or drop and recreate it.

ORA-01536.cause: The space quota for the tablespace of the object being created or extended has been exceeded.
ORA-01536.action: Ask the DBA to increase the user's tablespace quota: ALTER USER user QUOTA UNLIMITED ON tablespace;

ORA-01555.message: snapshot too old: rollback segment number %s with name "%s" too small
ORA-01555.cause: A query or fetch lasted longer than the undo retention period, or the undo tablespace was too small to retain undo data.
ORA-01555.action: Increase UNDO_RETENTION parameter. Increase undo tablespace size. Optimize long-running queries to complete faster. Consider using FLASHBACK features.

ORA-01562.message: failed to extend rollback segment number %s
ORA-01562.cause: The rollback or undo segment has reached its MAXEXTENTS limit or there is no space left in the tablespace.
ORA-01562.action: Increase MAXEXTENTS for the rollback segment, add space to the undo tablespace, or increase the undo tablespace autoextend setting.

ORA-01578.cause: The data block indicated is corrupted.
ORA-01578.action: Use RMAN block media recovery: RECOVER DATAFILE n BLOCK m; or restore from backup. Report to Oracle Support.

ORA-01591.message: lock held by in-doubt distributed transaction %s
ORA-01591.cause: An object is being locked by a distributed transaction that is in-doubt (two-phase commit problem).
ORA-01591.action: Force the in-doubt transaction: COMMIT FORCE 'transaction_id'; or ROLLBACK FORCE 'transaction_id'; Use DBA_2PC_PENDING to identify them.

ORA-01610.message: recovery using the BACKUP CONTROLFILE option must be done
ORA-01610.cause: Recovery using a backup control file requires the USING BACKUP CONTROLFILE clause.
ORA-01610.action: Use RECOVER DATABASE USING BACKUP CONTROLFILE.

ORA-01652.message: unable to extend temp segment by %s in tablespace %s
ORA-01652.cause: Failed to allocate enough space for a sort or hash operation in the TEMP tablespace.
ORA-01652.action: Add a temp file to the TEMP tablespace, increase the existing temp file size, or optimize the SQL to reduce sort requirements.

ORA-01653.message: unable to extend table %s.%s by %s in tablespace %s
ORA-01653.cause: The table cannot extend because there is no more space in the tablespace.
ORA-01653.action: Add a data file to the tablespace or enable autoextend. Alternatively, purge unnecessary data from the table.

ORA-01654.message: unable to extend index %s.%s by %s in tablespace %s
ORA-01654.cause: The index cannot extend because there is no more space in the tablespace.
ORA-01654.action: Add a data file to the tablespace or enable autoextend on existing data files.

ORA-01658.message: unable to create INITIAL extent for segment in tablespace %s
ORA-01658.cause: There is not enough contiguous free space in the tablespace to create the initial extent for the new segment.
ORA-01658.action: Add more space to the tablespace or coalesce free space.

ORA-01700.cause: A user is specified more than once in a GRANT or REVOKE statement.
ORA-01700.action: Remove duplicate usernames from the GRANT/REVOKE statement.

ORA-01722.cause: The specified number is invalid. This occurs when trying to convert a non-numeric string to a number.
ORA-01722.action: Check the data for non-numeric characters. Use REGEXP_LIKE to validate before conversion, or use CASE/WHEN to handle bad data.

ORA-01747.message: invalid user.table.column, table.column, or column specification
ORA-01747.cause: An invalid column specification was provided.

ORA-01756.cause: A quoted string was not terminated with a closing quote, or the closing quote was not a valid string terminator.
ORA-01756.action: Check the SQL statement for mismatched or missing quotes.

ORA-01779.message: cannot modify a column which maps to a non key-preserved table
ORA-01779.cause: An attempt was made to INSERT or UPDATE columns of a join view that map to a non-key-preserved table.
ORA-01779.action: Use an INSTEAD OF trigger or update the base tables directly.

ORA-01799.message: a column may not be outer-joined to a subquery
ORA-01799.cause: An outer join was attempted between a column and a subquery.
ORA-01799.action: Rewrite the query using an inline view or a LEFT OUTER JOIN with a proper subquery in the FROM clause.

ORA-02002.message: error while writing to audit trail
ORA-02002.cause: An error occurred while writing to the audit trail.
ORA-02002.action: Check the audit trail destination for space and permissions.

ORA-02049.message: timeout: distributed transaction waiting for lock
ORA-02049.cause: A timeout occurred in a distributed transaction waiting for a lock.
ORA-02049.action: Increase DISTRIBUTED_LOCK_TIMEOUT or investigate and remove the blocking distributed lock.

ORA-02055.message: distributed update operation failed; rollback required
ORA-02055.cause: A distributed update failed and requires rollback.

ORA-02063.cause: This message identifies the remote database that generated the preceding error.
ORA-02063.action: Look at the preceding error from the remote database to understand the issue.

ORA-02291.message: integrity constraint (%s.%s) violated - parent key not found
ORA-02291.cause: An attempt was made to insert or update a record with a foreign key value that does not exist in the parent table.
ORA-02291.action: Insert the parent record first, or check for typos in the foreign key value.

ORA-02292.message: integrity constraint (%s.%s) violated - child record found
ORA-02292.cause: An attempt was made to delete a parent record when child records exist in a foreign key relationship.
ORA-02292.action: Delete or update the child records first, or use ON DELETE CASCADE on the foreign key constraint.

ORA-02396.message: exceeded maximum idle time, please connect again
ORA-02396.cause: This session has been idle for too long and was disconnected per the profile's IDLE_TIME limit.
ORA-02396.action: Reconnect to the database. Ask the DBA to increase the IDLE_TIME profile parameter if needed.

ORA-02429.message: cannot drop index used for enforcement of unique/primary key
ORA-02429.cause: An attempt was made to drop an index that is being used to enforce a unique or primary key constraint.
ORA-02429.action: Disable or drop the constraint first, then drop the index.

ORA-02437.cause: Enabling a primary key constraint failed because duplicate values exist in the column(s).
ORA-02437.action: Remove duplicate values from the column before enabling the constraint.

ORA-02438.message: column check constraint cannot reference other columns
ORA-02438.cause: An attempt was made to define a column-level check constraint that references another column.
ORA-02438.action: Define the check constraint at the table level instead of the column level.

ORA-02443.cause: An attempt was made to drop a constraint that does not exist.
ORA-02443.action: Verify the constraint name using SELECT CONSTRAINT_NAME FROM USER_CONSTRAINTS;

ORA-03113.cause: The connection between the client and the database server was unexpectedly closed.
ORA-03113.action: Check the alert log and trace files. Verify the database is running. Check network stability between client and server.

ORA-03114.cause: The program is no longer connected to Oracle, or was never connected.
ORA-03114.action: Re-establish the database connection. Check the alert log for why the connection was dropped.

ORA-03135.cause: The connection between the client and server was lost, usually due to a network issue or server crash.
ORA-03135.action: Check network connectivity. Review the server alert log. Verify the database is up.

ORA-04020.message: deadlock detected while trying to lock object %s
ORA-04020.cause: A deadlock was detected when attempting to lock a library cache object.
ORA-04020.action: Retry the operation. If the error recurs, investigate whether concurrent DDL is causing contention.

ORA-04021.message: timeout occurred while waiting to lock object %s
ORA-04021.cause: A timeout occurred while waiting to lock a library cache object.
ORA-04021.action: Retry. If persistent, investigate library cache contention using V$LATCH and V$SESSION_WAIT.

ORA-04028.cause: Oracle was unable to generate the parse tree (DIANA) for the specified PL/SQL object.
ORA-04028.action: Recompile the object: ALTER PROCEDURE/PACKAGE/FUNCTION name COMPILE; If it fails, check for missing dependencies.

ORA-04031.message: unable to allocate %s bytes of shared memory (%s,%s,%s,%s)
ORA-04031.cause: More shared memory is needed than was allocated in the shared pool or large pool. Fragmentation may also be a cause.
ORA-04031.action: Use DBMS_SHARED_POOL.KEEP to pin large packages. Increase SHARED_POOL_SIZE. Set SHARED_POOL_RESERVED_SIZE. Flush the shared pool: ALTER SYSTEM FLUSH SHARED_POOL;

ORA-04036.message: PGA memory used by the instance exceeds PGA_AGGREGATE_LIMIT
ORA-04036.cause: The total PGA memory used by all sessions exceeds the PGA_AGGREGATE_LIMIT parameter.
ORA-04036.action: Increase PGA_AGGREGATE_LIMIT or PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET. Identify sessions with high PGA usage via V$PROCESS and V$SESSTAT.

ORA-04043.cause: The named object does not exist in the database, or you do not have privileges to view it.
ORA-04043.action: Check the object name spelling and schema. Use ALL_OBJECTS or DBA_OBJECTS to confirm existence.

ORA-04061.message: existing state of %s has been invalidated
ORA-04061.cause: The existing state (package global variables) of a package instance has been discarded because the package has been invalidated and recompiled.
ORA-04061.action: Re-initialize the package state. This is expected after DDL changes to the package. Handle PACKAGE_STATE_ERROR in application code.

ORA-04063.cause: An attempt was made to use a stored procedure, function, or package that has errors.
ORA-04063.action: Recompile the object and check for compilation errors: SELECT * FROM USER_ERRORS WHERE NAME = 'OBJECT_NAME';

ORA-04065.cause: A stored procedure or package was altered or dropped while it was being executed.
ORA-04065.action: Re-execute the calling program after the DDL operation is complete.

ORA-04067.cause: The stored procedure, function, or package referenced does not exist.
ORA-04067.action: Verify the object name and schema. Check ALL_OBJECTS for existence and validity.

ORA-04091.message: table %s.%s is mutating, trigger/function may not see it
ORA-04091.cause: A trigger (or a function called by a trigger) attempted to read or modify a table that is currently being modified by the triggering statement.
ORA-04091.action: Use compound triggers (Oracle 11g+) or autonomous transactions to work around the mutating table restriction.

ORA-05555.message: time stamp mismatch - check ORACLE_HOME settings
ORA-05555.action: Verify ORACLE_HOME is consistent across all Oracle components.

ORA-06502.cause: An arithmetic, numeric, string, conversion, or constraint error occurred in PL/SQL.
ORA-06502.action: Check variable sizes and data types. Ensure assignments don't exceed variable constraints. Add explicit conversion and validation.

ORA-06504.message: PL/SQL: Return types of Result Set variables or query do not match
ORA-06504.cause: The return type of the cursor variable does not match the return type of the query.
ORA-06504.action: Ensure the cursor variable type matches the SELECT statement columns.

ORA-06508.message: PL/SQL: could not find program unit being called: "%s"
ORA-06508.cause: The specified program unit does not exist or has not been compiled successfully.
ORA-06508.action: Verify the package/procedure/function exists and is valid: SELECT STATUS FROM ALL_OBJECTS WHERE OBJECT_NAME = 'NAME';

ORA-06512.cause: Backtrace message as the exception propagated through the call stack.
ORA-06512.action: This line identifies the location of the error in the PL/SQL call stack. Look at the preceding error for the root cause.

ORA-06550.action: Fix the PL/SQL syntax error at the indicated line and column.

ORA-07445.cause: An operating system exception occurred which should result in the creation of a core file. This is an Oracle internal error.
ORA-07445.action: Contact Oracle Support and provide the trace file and core dump.

ORA-08002.message: sequence %s.CURRVAL is not yet defined in this session
ORA-08002.cause: CURRVAL was referenced before NEXTVAL was called in this session.
ORA-08002.action: Call sequence.NEXTVAL before using sequence.CURRVAL in the same session.

ORA-08003.message: sequence %s.NEXTVAL exceeds MAXVALUE and cannot be instantiated
ORA-08003.cause: The sequence has reached its MAXVALUE and NOCYCLE is set.
ORA-08003.action: ALTER SEQUENCE name CYCLE or ALTER SEQUENCE name MAXVALUE new_max or recreate the sequence.

ORA-08004.cause: NEXTVAL would violate the sequence's MINVALUE or MAXVALUE.
ORA-08004.action: Alter the sequence parameters to accommodate the required range.

ORA-09817.cause: Oracle was unable to write to the audit trail file.
ORA-09817.action: Check disk space on the audit file destination. Verify file system permissions for the Oracle OS user.

ORA-10173.cause: Dynamic sampling exceeded the time limit and was aborted.
ORA-10173.action: Gather statistics on the affected objects. Reduce OPTIMIZER_DYNAMIC_SAMPLING or add a NO_DYNAMIC_SAMPLING hint.

ORA-10458.cause: The standby database is not in sync with the primary.
ORA-10458.action: Apply archived logs or redo to the standby. Check Data Guard status.

ORA-10632.action: Verify the ROWID value. It may have been corrupted or the row may have been moved.

ORA-11001.action: Contact Oracle Support with the trace file.

ORA-12012.message: error on auto execute of job %s
ORA-12012.cause: The Oracle Scheduler job failed during auto-execute.
ORA-12012.action: Check DBA_SCHEDULER_JOB_RUN_DETAILS for the specific error. Review the job's stored procedure for errors.

ORA-12154.message: TNS:could not resolve the connect identifier specified
ORA-12154.cause: A connection was requested using a connect identifier that could not be resolved. TNS alias is not defined in tnsnames.ora or LDAP.
ORA-12154.action: Check tnsnames.ora for the correct alias. Verify ORACLE_HOME/network/admin path. Test with tnsping. Check TNS_ADMIN environment variable.

ORA-12170.cause: The client failed to establish a connection within the timeout period.
ORA-12170.action: Check network connectivity to the host. Verify the listener is running. Check for firewalls blocking the port. Increase SQLNET.INBOUND_CONNECT_TIMEOUT.

ORA-12203.cause: TNS was unable to connect to the specified destination.
ORA-12203.action: Verify the host, port, and service name. Check that the listener is running: lsnrctl status.

ORA-12224.cause: The connection request could not be completed because the listener is not running.
ORA-12224.action: Start the Oracle listener: lsnrctl start. Verify the listener.ora configuration.

ORA-12505.message: TNS:listener does not currently know of SID given in connect descriptor
ORA-12505.cause: The listener received a request to establish a connection to a database or other service. The connect descriptor received by the listener specified a SID that the listener does not know about.
ORA-12505.action: Check the SID in the connection string. Ensure the database is registered with the listener. Use SERVICE_NAME instead of SID in newer Oracle versions.

ORA-12514.message: TNS:listener does not currently know of service requested in connect descriptor
ORA-12514.cause: The listener received a connection request but does not know the service name in the connect descriptor.
ORA-12514.action: Verify the SERVICE_NAME in the connection string matches the database service. Check lsnrctl services output. Restart the listener after DB startup.

ORA-12516.message: TNS:listener could not find available handler with matching protocol stack
ORA-12516.cause: The listener could not find an available handler with matching protocol stack for the requesting client.
ORA-12516.action: Increase PROCESSES and SESSIONS initialization parameters. Check for connection pool exhaustion.

ORA-12519.cause: The listener cannot find an available and appropriate handler for the request.
ORA-12519.action: Increase the PROCESSES parameter. Check connection pool usage. Verify the dispatchers are running if using shared server mode.

ORA-12528.message: TNS:listener: all appropriate instances are blocking new connections
ORA-12528.cause: All database instances that handle the service are blocking new connections, usually because the database is starting up.
ORA-12528.action: Wait for the database to finish startup. If the database is in restricted mode, alter it: ALTER SYSTEM DISABLE RESTRICTED SESSION;

ORA-12541.cause: Attempted to connect to a host on which the Oracle listener is not running.
ORA-12541.action: Start the listener: lsnrctl start. Verify the host and port in the connection descriptor.

ORA-12560.cause: A generic TNS protocol adapter error. Often means the Oracle service is not running on the local machine (Windows).
ORA-12560.action: On Windows: start the OracleService via services.msc. On Linux: start the instance with sqlplus / as sysdba and STARTUP. Check ORACLE_SID is set correctly.

ORA-12571.cause: An error occurred when sending data to the Oracle server.
ORA-12571.action: Check network stability. Review sqlnet.log for details. Investigate client-server network path.

ORA-12592.cause: A malformed packet was received on the network.
ORA-12592.action: Check network stability. Review Oracle Net log files.

ORA-12637.cause: Oracle Net received a corrupted or unexpected packet.
ORA-12637.action: Check network integrity. Review sqlnet.log on both client and server.

ORA-12638.cause: Oracle Net was unable to retrieve the user's credentials from the network authentication service.
ORA-12638.action: Check the SQLNET.AUTHENTICATION_SERVICES setting in sqlnet.ora. If OS authentication is not required, set it to NONE.

ORA-12899.message: value too large for column %s (actual: %s, maximum: %s)
ORA-12899.cause: An INSERT or UPDATE supplied a value that is too large for the column definition.
ORA-12899.action: Truncate the value to fit the column, or use ALTER TABLE to widen the column.

ORA-13516.cause: The AWR (Automatic Workload Repository) operation failed.
ORA-13516.action: Check the AWR tablespace (SYSAUX) for space. Run DBMS_WORKLOAD_REPOSITORY.AWR_REPORT_HTML to diagnose. Check for SYSAUX corruption.

ORA-14400.message: inserted partition key does not map to any partition
ORA-14400.cause: An INSERT was attempted into a partitioned table but the partition key value does not fall within any defined partition range.
ORA-14400.action: Add a partition that covers the new key value, or use INTERVAL partitioning to auto-create partitions.

ORA-14402.message: updating partition key column would cause a partition change
ORA-14402.cause: An UPDATE changed the value of a partition key column so the row would need to move to a different partition, but ROW MOVEMENT is not enabled.
ORA-14402.action: Enable row movement: ALTER TABLE table_name ENABLE ROW MOVEMENT; then retry.

ORA-14551.message: cannot perform a DML operation inside a query
ORA-14551.cause: A DML operation (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) was attempted inside a query context where it is not permitted.
ORA-14551.action: Use an autonomous transaction pragma (PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION) if the DML must occur in a function called from SQL.

ORA-14752.message: Interval expression is not a constant of the correct type
ORA-14752.cause: The INTERVAL expression used in partition definition is not a valid constant.
ORA-14752.action: Use a valid interval constant such as NUMTOYMINTERVAL(1,'MONTH') or NUMTODSINTERVAL(1,'DAY').

ORA-17008.cause: An attempt was made to use a closed database connection.
ORA-17008.action: Re-establish the connection before executing SQL. Check connection pool health.

ORA-17009.cause: An attempt was made to use a statement that has already been closed.
ORA-17009.action: Re-prepare the statement before executing. Check for premature statement.close() calls in JDBC/OCI code.

ORA-17011.cause: An attempt was made to fetch beyond the end of a result set.
ORA-17011.action: Check the loop condition when iterating over result sets. Use ResultSet.next() return value to control iteration.

ORA-17068.cause: An invalid argument was passed to an Oracle JDBC or OCI call.
ORA-17068.action: Review the API call and ensure all parameters are valid and within expected ranges.

ORA-17410.message: No more data to read from socket
ORA-17410.cause: The JDBC driver could not read more data from the socket.
ORA-17410.action: Check network stability. Investigate database-side issues that may have caused the connection to drop.

ORA-17447.message: OALL8 is in an inconsistent state
ORA-17447.cause: The OCI layer (OALL8) is in an inconsistent state, usually caused by a previous error not being handled properly.
ORA-17447.action: Close the connection and open a fresh one. This usually follows another error that was not handled correctly.

ORA-19502.message: write error on file "%s", block number %s
ORA-19502.cause: A write error occurred on the specified file during backup or recovery.
ORA-19502.action: Check disk space on the backup destination. Verify file system permissions. Check OS I/O error logs.

ORA-19505.cause: RMAN or Oracle could not identify the specified backup file.
ORA-19505.action: Verify the file path and that the file exists. Re-catalog the backup if needed: RMAN> CATALOG DATAFILECOPY 'path';

ORA-19625.cause: RMAN could not identify the specified backup piece.
ORA-19625.action: Verify the backup file path. Use RMAN LIST BACKUP to check available backups. Recatalog if missing.

ORA-20000.message: ORU-10027: buffer overflow, limit of %s bytes
ORA-20000.action: Increase the buffer size: DBMS_OUTPUT.ENABLE(1000000); or reduce the amount of output generated.

ORA-22922.cause: An attempt was made to access a LOB value that does not exist.
ORA-22922.action: Verify the LOB locator is valid and the LOB column has been populated.

ORA-24000.cause: The session is not connected to an Oracle database.
ORA-24000.action: Establish a database connection before executing SQL.

ORA-24237.message: object id argument to DBMS_UTILITY.INVALIDATE is not that of an existing object
ORA-24237.cause: The object ID passed to DBMS_UTILITY.INVALIDATE does not correspond to an existing object.
ORA-24237.action: Verify the object ID using SELECT OBJECT_ID FROM DBA_OBJECTS WHERE OBJECT_NAME = 'NAME';

ORA-24816.message: Expanded non LONG bind data supplied after actual LONG or LOB column
ORA-24816.cause: An attempt was made to supply non-LONG bind data in a position after a LONG or LOB column.
ORA-24816.action: Reorder the bind variables or columns so LONG and LOB columns come last.

ORA-25153.cause: The temporary tablespace has no temp files.
ORA-25153.action: Add a tempfile: ALTER TABLESPACE TEMP ADD TEMPFILE '/path/temp01.dbf' SIZE 1G AUTOEXTEND ON;

ORA-25400.cause: The operation requires replaying a fetch due to a failover scenario.
ORA-25400.action: Re-execute the query from the beginning of the result set.

ORA-25401.cause: Fetching could not continue, usually because the cursor has been invalidated.
ORA-25401.action: Close and reopen the cursor. Re-execute the query.

ORA-25402.cause: The transaction must be rolled back due to a failed operation.
ORA-25402.action: Issue a ROLLBACK statement and retry the transaction.

ORA-25403.cause: The application requested a reconnect but it failed.
ORA-25403.action: Establish a fresh connection to the database.

ORA-25404.cause: The instance was lost, usually due to a RAC node failure.
ORA-25404.action: Check RAC cluster health. Reconnect to a surviving instance.

ORA-28000.cause: The user account has been locked after too many failed login attempts.
ORA-28000.action: Unlock the account: ALTER USER username ACCOUNT UNLOCK; Review the FAILED_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS profile parameter.

ORA-28001.cause: The user's password has expired per the password lifetime setting in the profile.
ORA-28001.action: Change the password: ALTER USER username IDENTIFIED BY new_password; or ALTER PROFILE profile LIMIT PASSWORD_LIFE_TIME UNLIMITED;

ORA-28002.message: the password will expire within %s days
ORA-28002.cause: The user's password is about to expire.
ORA-28002.action: Change the password before it expires to avoid being locked out.

ORA-28003.message: password verification for the specified password failed
ORA-28003.cause: The new password did not meet the requirements of the password verify function.
ORA-28003.action: Choose a password that meets the complexity requirements defined in the password verify function.

ORA-28009.message: connection as SYS should be as SYSDBA or SYSOPER
ORA-28009.cause: An attempt was made to connect as SYS without specifying SYSDBA or SYSOPER.
ORA-28009.action: Connect using: sqlplus sys/password@db AS SYSDBA

ORA-28040.cause: The client and server cannot agree on an authentication protocol. Usually a version mismatch.
ORA-28040.action: Set SQLNET.ALLOWED_LOGON_VERSION_SERVER and CLIENT in sqlnet.ora to allow older clients. Update client drivers to a compatible version.

ORA-28113.cause: A Virtual Private Database (VPD) policy function returned an invalid predicate.
ORA-28113.action: Review the VPD policy function for errors. Check USER_ERRORS for compilation issues in the policy function.

ORA-28115.cause: An INSERT or UPDATE violated a VPD policy with check option enabled.
ORA-28115.action: Review the VPD policy to understand why the row was rejected. Adjust the data to comply with the policy predicate.

ORA-28500.message:
connection from ORACLE to a non-Oracle system returned this message:
[%s]
ORA-28500.cause: A heterogeneous services (database link to non-Oracle DB) operation returned an error from the remote system.
ORA-28500.action: Check the HS agent log. Verify connectivity to the non-Oracle data source.

ORA-28547.message: connection to server failed, probable Oracle Net admin error
ORA-28547.cause: Oracle Net was unable to connect to the server, usually due to misconfiguration.
ORA-28547.action: Review sqlnet.ora and tnsnames.ora. Run tnsping to test connectivity. Check the listener log.

ORA-29275.cause: A partial multi-byte character was found during character set conversion.
ORA-29275.action: Ensure data is stored in a complete multi-byte character encoding. Check for data corruption or truncation.

ORA-29283.cause: An invalid file operation was attempted using UTL_FILE.
ORA-29283.action: Verify the directory object path and permissions. Check that the file path uses a valid Oracle directory object.

ORA-29285.cause: An error occurred during a UTL_FILE write operation.
ORA-29285.action: Check disk space on the directory. Verify write permissions on the file and directory.

ORA-29292.cause: UTL_FILE.FRENAME was called with invalid parameters.
ORA-29292.action: Verify both source and destination directory objects and file names are valid.

ORA-30036.message: unable to extend segment by %s in undo tablespace '%s'
ORA-30036.cause: The undo tablespace has run out of space.
ORA-30036.action: Add a data file to the undo tablespace or enable autoextend. Consider reducing UNDO_RETENTION or committing transactions more frequently.

ORA-30926.message: unable to get a stable set of rows in the source tables
ORA-30926.cause: A MERGE statement was unable to get a stable set of source rows, usually because the source query returns duplicate rows for a single target row.
ORA-30926.action: Ensure the source query returns at most one row per target row. Use aggregation or DISTINCT in the source query.

ORA-31011.cause: Oracle could not parse the provided XML data.
ORA-31011.action: Validate the XML against its schema. Check for malformed XML, encoding issues, or missing namespace declarations.

ORA-31626.cause: A Data Pump operation referenced a job that does not exist.
ORA-31626.action: List active Data Pump jobs: SELECT JOB_NAME, STATE FROM DBA_DATAPUMP_JOBS; Verify the job name.

ORA-32004.message: obsolete or deprecated parameter(s) specified for RDBMS instance
ORA-32004.cause: An obsolete initialization parameter was specified.
ORA-32004.action: Remove the obsolete parameter from the PFILE/SPFILE. Check Oracle documentation for the current replacement parameter.

ORA-32017.cause: An error occurred when attempting to update the server parameter file.
ORA-32017.action: Check that the SPFILE exists and is accessible. Verify permissions on the SPFILE location.

ORA-32019.message: cannot update parameter to value specified for server parameter file update
ORA-32019.cause: The parameter value is invalid for SPFILE update.
ORA-32019.action: Check the valid range for the parameter. Use ALTER SYSTEM ... SCOPE=MEMORY first to test, then SCOPE=SPFILE.

ORA-38301.message: can not perform DDL/DML over objects in Recycle Bin
ORA-38301.cause: An attempt was made to perform DDL or DML on an object that is in the Recycle Bin.
ORA-38301.action: Either restore the object with FLASHBACK TABLE name TO BEFORE DROP, or purge it: PURGE TABLE name;

ORA-38760.message: This database instance failed to turn on flashback database logging
ORA-38760.cause: Flashback logging could not be enabled, usually due to insufficient space in the Fast Recovery Area.
ORA-38760.action: Increase DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST_SIZE or add more space to the FRA location.

ORA-39000.cause: A Data Pump operation was given an invalid dump file specification.
ORA-39000.action: Verify the DIRECTORY object and DUMPFILE parameter in the Data Pump job.

ORA-39001.cause: A Data Pump API call was given an invalid argument.
ORA-39001.action: Review the DBMS_DATAPUMP API call parameters for correctness.

ORA-39002.cause: An invalid operation was requested in a Data Pump job.
ORA-39002.action: Review the Data Pump documentation for valid operations in the current job state.

ORA-39006.cause: An internal Data Pump error occurred.
ORA-39006.action: Check the Data Pump log file. Contact Oracle Support with the log and trace files.

ORA-39014.message: One or more workers have prematurely exited.
ORA-39014.cause: One or more Data Pump worker processes terminated unexpectedly.
ORA-39014.action: Check the Data Pump log file and the alert log for errors. Re-attach to the job and attempt to restart it.

ORA-39083.message:
Object type %s failed to create with error:
%s
ORA-39083.cause: A Data Pump import failed to create the specified object type.
ORA-39083.action: Review the error detail. The object may already exist, have missing dependencies, or require additional privileges.

ORA-39126.cause: A Data Pump worker process encountered a fatal unexpected error.
ORA-39126.action: Check the Data Pump log and alert log. Contact Oracle Support with trace files.

ORA-40478.cause: The output of an AI Vector Search or text mining function exceeded the maximum size.
ORA-40478.action: Reduce the input or use a different output format.

ORA-44787.cause: Attempted to suspend an Oracle service that cannot be suspended.

ORA-47400.cause: A Database Vault command rule prevented the operation.
ORA-47400.action: Review Database Vault command rules with your security administrator.

ORA-54013.message: INSERT operation disallowed on virtual columns
ORA-54013.cause: An INSERT statement attempted to specify a value for a virtual column.
ORA-54013.action: Remove virtual columns from the INSERT column list. Virtual columns are computed automatically.

ORA-55302.message: defaulting to undo tablespace for flashback data archive
ORA-55302.cause: No specific Flashback Data Archive tablespace was specified.
ORA-55302.action: Specify a dedicated tablespace for the Flashback Data Archive to avoid UNDO tablespace pressure.

ORA-65040.message: operation not allowed from within a pluggable database
ORA-65040.cause: An operation that is only allowed at the CDB root level was attempted from a PDB.
ORA-65040.action: Connect to the CDB root (ALTER SESSION SET CONTAINER = CDB$ROOT;) to perform this operation.

ORA-65048.message: error encountered when switching into pluggable database %s
ORA-65048.cause: An error occurred when switching to the specified PDB.
ORA-65048.action: Check that the PDB is open: SELECT NAME, OPEN_MODE FROM V$PDBS; Open it if closed: ALTER PLUGGABLE DATABASE pdb_name OPEN;

ORA-65090.message: operation not allowed in a non-CDB database
ORA-65090.cause: A CDB-specific operation was attempted on a non-CDB (traditional) database.
ORA-65090.action: This feature requires a multitenant/CDB architecture. Use the standard equivalent for non-CDB databases.
